response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an accomplished full-stack engineer with strong TypeScript/Node.js and DLang expertise, but he does not meet the mandatory requirements for this role. His C++ exposure is limited to approximately six months at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a bachelor thesis project — far short of the required 5–10 years of hands-on C++ on Windows. There is no evidence of SQL Server or Oracle experience (MongoDB is his primary database), no Summit platform exposure, and while he spent time at RIDE Capital, that role was Node.js/TypeScript development with no traceable fixed-income or derivatives domain knowledge. The gap between his profile and the mandatory requirements is too wide to bridge with optional strengths.
strengths: Broad polyglot engineering background with demonstrated ability to work across multiple languages and platforms (TypeScript, DLang, Java, C, C++)
Capital-markets-adjacent experience at RIDE Capital, showing some familiarity with the fintech/investment domain even if not fixed income specifically
Strong CI/CD, DevOps, and software-craftsmanship practices (TDD, trunk-based development, GitLab CI, Docker) that align with desirable optional skills
concerns: C++ experience is negligible (~6 months in 2014–2015 plus a thesis project) versus the mandatory 5–10 years required for the role
No SQL Server or Oracle experience; primary database background is MongoDB and MySQL at a non-enterprise scale, with no capital-markets data-tier work
Zero fixed-income, fixed-income derivatives, or investment-banking domain knowledge; RIDE Capital involvement was purely application-layer TypeScript development

key_factors.factor: No fixed income or capital markets domain knowledge
